title: SSC CGL 2026 Sectional Timing Tier I 22 22

questions.question: There are five people sitting in a linear row and all are facing in the north direction. Each one of them has different number of shirts. Only one person sits to the left of S, who has 7 shirts. R, who has one shirt less than S, sits second to the right of S. P, who has 2 shirts less than R, is not an immediate neighbor of R. Q, who has one shirt more than P, is not an immediate neighbor of S. T has 3 shirts. Who among the following sits second to the right of T?
questions.explanation: Q, who has 5 shirts, sits second to the right of T. Detailed solution · Only one person sits to the left of S, who has 7 shirts. · R, who has one shirt less than S, sits second to the right of S. · So, R has 6 shirts. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p14333/image026.png) · P, who has 2 shirts less than R, is not an immediate neighbor of R. · So, P has 4 shirts. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p14333/image027.png) · Q, who has one shirt more than P, is not an immediate neighbor of S. · So, Q has 5 shirts. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p14333/image028.png) · T has 3 shirts. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p14333/image029.png) Finally, we get,

questions.question: A and B can do a piece of work in 15 days and 10 days respectively. A works for 5 days alone then B joins him. For how many days will they work together?
questions.explanation: Work done by A in 5 days = 5/15 = 1/3 Remaining work = 1 - 1/3 = 2/3 Total work done by A and B in a day = (1/15) + (1/10) = 5/30 = 1/6 Time taken by A and B to complete 2/3 of work = 6 X (2/3) = 4 days

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the third word in the same way as the second word is related to the first word. (The words must be considered as meaningful English words and must not be related to each other based on the number of letters/consonants/vowels in the word). Chess : Illegal Move :: Exam : ?
questions.explanation: An illegal move is a rule violation in chess. Cheating is a rule violation in an exam.

questions.question: Select the triad which follows the same pattern as that followed by the two triads given below. Both triads follow the same pattern. CQ - 54 - JX HM - 56 - OT
Select the option that shows the given words arranged as they appear in an English dictionary. 1) Decadent 2) Decimal 3) Declare 4) Decoy 5) Decathlon
questions.explanation: 1) Decadent 5) Decathlon 2) Decimal 3) Declare 4) Decoy So, the dictionary order is (1), (5), (2), (3), (4).

questions.question: What comes next in the series? D, V, G, S, K, O, ?
questions.explanation: Pattern for odd positions: D + 3 = G G + 4 = K K + 5 = P Pattern for even positions: V − 3 = S S − 4 = O So, the next term in the series is P.

questions.question: Consider the following statement and choose the option that follows. Statement: The health department will provide free booster doses at community centres from 15 January to 31 January. Conclusions: I. Booster doses will be mandatory for all citizens. II. People can take booster doses at those centres without paying during that period.
options: Neither conclusion I nor II follows
Both conclusions I and II follow
questions.explanation: Conclusion I does not follow because "free" does not mean "mandatory." Conclusion II follows because the statement clearly says booster doses will be free at community centres during the given dates.

questions.question: What comes next: 6, 8, 18, 48, 116, ?
After interchanging + and X and also interchanging 8 and 13, which of the following equations will hold true?
questions.explanation: a. (13+4) X 8=90 After interchanging: (8 X 4)+13 = 32+13 = 45 b. (8+6) X 13=112 After interchanging: (13 X 6)+8 = 78+8 = 86 c. (8+3) X 13=47 After interchanging: (13 X 3)+8 = 39+8 = 47 d. (13+2) X 8=80 After interchanging: (8 X 2)+13 = 16+13 = 29 Hence, option 'c' is correct.

questions.question: The question given below consists of a statement, followed by two arguments numbered I and II. You have to decide which of the arguments is a 'strong' argument and which is a 'weak' argument. Statement: Should all court trials be live-streamed on television and the internet? Argument I: Yes, because many people enjoy watching serious debates like a daily show. Argument II: No, it can affect witness safety and fair trial conditions; limited broadcasting with strict safeguards may be more appropriate.
options: Both arguments I and II are strong
Neither argument I nor II is strong
questions.explanation: Argument I focuses on entertainment value, which is not a serious or relevant basis for justice policy. Argument II directly addresses fairness and safety concerns and offers a balanced alternative, so it is strong. So, only Argument II is strong.

questions.question: Choose the address that is exactly the same as the one given below. D-59, 2nd Floor, Pratap Market, Munirka, New Delhi - 110067
options: D-59, 2nd Floor, Pratap Market, Munirka, New Delhi - 110067
D-59, 2nd Floor, Pratap Market, Munikra, New Delhi - 110067
D-59, Second Floor, Pratap Market, Munirka, New Delhi - 110067
D-59, 2nd Floor, Pratap Market, Munirka, New Delhi - 110076
questions.explanation: Option a, matches the given address exactly in spelling, ordinal format "2nd", dash style, and PIN. Option b, misspells "Munirka" as "Munikra". Option c, changes "2nd" to "Second". Option d, changes the PIN code to 110076

questions.question: Four numbers have been given out of which three are alike in some manner, while one is different. Choose the odd one.
questions.explanation: All the three numbers except 117 are prime numbers. 117 is divisible by 9 and 13.

questions.question: H is granddaughter of A's mother's mother. S is L's grandson and child of F's daughter's son, who is A. H has one brother. How is L related to F?
questions.explanation: S is child of F's daughter's son, who is A and S is L's grandson. We get, ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p8187/image010.jpg) ≫ S is L's grandson and, ≫ H is granddaughter of A's mother's mother. Also, H has one brother. Thus, A must be H's brother and L is A's mother and F is L's mother. We get, ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p8187/image011.jpg) Hence, L is F's daughter.

questions.question: Select the letter cluster from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series. KJZM, KLYN, KNWO, KPTP, ?, KTKR, KVES
questions.explanation: KJZM KJ+2= LZ-1= YN KL+2=NY-2= WO KN+2=PW-3= TP KP+2= RT-4= PQ KR+2=TP-5= KR KT+2= VK-6= ES So, 'KRPQ' will replace the question mark (?) in the following series.

questions.question: In the following question, which one set of letters when sequentially placed at the gaps in the given letter series shall complete it? q q _ p m _ y q _ p p _ m _ q p p _ m _ y
options: q, p, q, m, y, p, m
q, y, q, m, y, p, m
q, m, q, p, y, p, m
q, m, q, m, y, p, m
questions.explanation: In each repetition one 'q' is deleted and one 'p' is added in the sequence 'qqqpmmy'. q q q p m m y q q p p m m y q p p p m m y So, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Find the missing term (?) in the following series. 86, 112, 147, 193, ?, 326
questions.explanation: The logic for the above series is: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p28682/image001.png) Hence, the missing term is 252.

questions.question: Introducing Chetan, Abhuday said, "He is the son of my wife's daughter's husband." How is Abhuday related to Chetan?
questions.explanation: Abhuday said, "He is the son of my wife's daughter's husband." ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p22266/image020.png) Abhuday is the maternal grandfather of Chetan.

questions.question: You are leading a cleanliness drive in a public park. A sponsor offers extra funds if your team places their large promotional banners on children's play equipment for photos. What should you do?
options: Accept; visibility is vital for future funding.
Accept but only after the event is over when children leave.
Decline the condition, propose alternative safe placements (perimeter fences, stalls), and proceed with the drive.
Decline the funds and cancel the drive if they insist.
questions.explanation: Safety and public utility take precedence. Offering safe alternative placements respects both ethics and partnership. Accepting banners on equipment endangers children and misuses public spaces. Doing it later doesn't fix misuse. Canceling outright is excessive if a reasonable alternative exists.

questions.question: In a certain code language, 'PARTY' is written as '172181924' and 'LIGHT' is written as '13107719'. How will 'ABOVE' be written in that language?
questions.explanation: 'PARTY' is written as '172181924'. Here, the pattern is: P+1=Q and the place value of Q=17. A+1=B and the place value of B=2. The place value of R=18. T-1=S and the place value of S=19. Y-1=X and the place value of X=24. 'LIGHT' is written as '13107719'. L+1=M and the place value of M=13. I+1=J and the place value of J=10 The place value of G=7. H-1=G and the place value of G=7. T-1=S and the place value of S=19. Similarly, A+1=B and the place value of B=2. B+1=C and the place value of C=3. The place value of O is 15. V-1=U and the place value of U=21. E-1=D and the place value of D=4. ABCDEFGHIJKLM 12345678910111213 ZYXWVUTSRQPON 26252423222120191817161514

questions.question: In the following question below are given some statements followed by some conclusions based on those statements. Take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts. Read all the conclusions and then decide which of the given conclusions logically follows the given statements. Statements: All L are O. All O are N. No L are M. Conclusions: I. All N are L. II. No N are O. III. Some N are M
options: Only conclusions II and III follow
Only conclusions I and III follow
questions.explanation: It is given that, All L are O. All O are N. No L are M. The basic minimum overlapping and another possible diagrams are, ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p21471/image015.jpg) Here, some N are L, some N are O, and some N can be M. Hence, none of the conclusions follow.

questions.question: How many alphabet positions will not change if the letters in the word 'ECLAMPSY' are arranged in alphabetical order from left to right?
questions.explanation: The given word is ECLAMPSY. If all letters are arranged in alphabetical order from left to right, then we get: Original lettersECLAMPSY Alphabetical orderACELMPSY So, the position of 5 letters will remain not changed.

questions.question: Which two numbers from among the following should be interchanged to make the given equation correct? 180 ÷ 5 + 15 X (27 − 12) − 8 = 172
In a particular code language, if DOMESTIC is written as PFUD in code format, how will INDUCTOR be written?
questions.explanation: We know that, ABCDEFGHIJKLM 12345678910111213 ZYXWVUTSRQPON 26252423222120191817161514 Here, the pattern is: The 1st, 3rd, 5th and 7th letters are dropped and the remaining letters are replaced by immediate next alphabet as in the English alphabetical series. Thus, in DOMESTIC - D, M, S and I are dropped. Then, O + 1 = P, E + 1 = F, T + 1 = U, C + 1 = D. Similarly, In INDUCTOR, I, D, C and O are dropped. Then, N + 1 = O, U + 1 = V, T + 1 = U, R + 1 = S.

questions.question: In football, what is the term used when a player scores three goals in a single match?
questions.explanation: • "Hat-trick" refers to when a player scores three goals in one match. • "Offside" is a rule where a player is positioned too close to the opponent's goal. • "Corner Kick" is awarded when the ball crosses the goal line after touching a defender. • "Penalty Kick" is awarded for a foul in the penalty area. Further Insight: • Football Important Terminologies: ○ ○ Offside - A player is in an offside position if they are nearer to the opponent's goal line than both the ball and the second-last defender. ○ Free Kick - Awarded after a foul, can be direct or indirect. ○ Corner Kick - A kick awarded to the attacking team when the ball crosses the goal line after being touched by a defending player. ○ Penalty Kick - Awarded when a foul occurs within the penalty box. ○ Hat-trick - When a player scores three goals in a single match. ○ Yellow/Red Card - Disciplinary measures for misconduct.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the judiciary in India is correct? I.The Supreme Court's decisions are binding on all other courts in India. II.A judge of the Supreme Court can be removed by the President on his own discretion. III.The concept of Public Interest Litigation (PIL) originated in the UK.
questions.explanation: Only statement I is correct. Statement I is correct: According to Article 141 of the Indian Constitution, the law declared by the Supreme Court shall be binding on all courts within the territory of India. This establishes the Supreme Court as the apex judicial authority. Explanation of Incorrect Statements: Statement II is incorrect: A judge of the Supreme Court can be removed only through a complex process of impeachment. The President can issue the removal order only after an address by Parliament has been presented to him, supported by a special majority of each House (a majority of the total membership of that House and a majority of not less than two-thirds of the members of that House present and voting). The President cannot act on his own discretion. Statement III is incorrect: The concept of Public Interest Litigation (PIL) originated and developed in the USA in the 1960s. It was introduced into the Indian judicial system in the late 1970s and early 1980s.

questions.question: Evaluate the following about Hindustani instruments and choose the correct option: Statement 1: The Sitar is a plucked string instrument with sympathetic strings that resonate to create a rich, shimmering sound. Statement 2: The Tabla is a pair of small hand drums; the smaller drum, the Dayan, is used for treble notes, while the larger Bayan produces bass.
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The Sitar is one of the most famous Indian string instruments, and its distinctive sound is largely due to its many sympathetic strings running underneath the main frets. Statement 2 is correct. The Tabla consists of two drums played together. The Dayan (right-hand drum) is tuned to a specific note and produces higher-pitched sounds, while the Bayan (left-hand drum) provides the bass and deeper tones.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is/are correct? I. The Quit India Movement was launched at the session of the All-India Congress Committee in Mumbai in 1942. II. Jawaharlal Nehru gave the call 'Do or Die' during the Quit India Movement. III. Quit India Movement is also known as the August Kranti. Select the correct answer.
questions.explanation: · Statement III is correct: Quit India Movement is also known as the India August Movement or August Kranti. · Statement I is correct: It was launched by Gandhiji at the session of the All-India Congress Committee in Mumbai in 1942. · Statement II is incorrect: Gandhiji gave the call 'Do or Die' in his speech delivered at the Gowalia Tank Maidan, popularly known as August Kranti Maidan. · The slogan 'Quit India' was coined by Yusuf Meherally, a socialist and trade unionist who also served as Mayor of Mumbai.

questions.question: Which of the following statements are correct regarding the Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY)? 1.It is a placement-linked skill training initiative for rural youth from poor families. 2.It is part of the National Rural Livelihoods Mission (NRLM). 3.It was launched on 25 September 2014. 4.The scheme is open to all youth residing in rural areas, regardless of their family's economic status.
questions.explanation: Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY) is the demand-driven placement-linked skill training initiative of the Ministry of Rural Development1 (MoRD), Government of India, uniquely aimed at rural poor youth between 15 and 35 years of age, with the purpose to create income diversity in poor families and help rural youth realize their career aspirations. It is a part of the National Rural Livelihood Mission (NRLM) - the Mission for poverty reduction called Aajeevika. DDU-GKY was launched on 25 September 2014. The scheme will benefit more than 55 million poor rural youth who are ready to be skilled by providing sustainable employment. Statement 4 is incorrect. This statement is the key error. The scheme is not universal for all rural youth. Its primary target is youth from poor and marginalized families, identified through criteria like BPL lists, MGNREGA worker households, or households part of the Rashtriya Swasthya Bima Yojana (RSBY). Economic status is a fundamental eligibility criterion.

questions.question: The term 'natural increase of population' refers to the increase in population due to:
options: The difference between birth rates and death rates.
questions.explanation: Natural increase of population is calculated by subtracting the death rate from the birth rate. It does not include migration. So, it's the difference between births and deaths in a population over a period of time.

questions.question: As per Industrial Policy of 1991, the government also allowed foreign companies to hold __________ share or more in case of their collaboration with Indian companies so that they can function freely and as the owner.
questions.explanation: In 1991 industrial policy, India adopted soft attitude towards foreign companies to do their business in India in order to promote competition. It also committed itself to abolish or reduce tariff on import of commodities. · The government also allowed foreign companies to hold 51 percent share or more in case of their collaboration with Indian companies so that they can function freely and as the owner. Further insights: The New Industrial Policy of 1991 aimed to dismantle the "License Raj" and liberalize the Indian economy. Key features included the abolition of industrial licensing for most industries, opening up many sectors previously reserved for the public sector to private investment, and liberalizing foreign investment and technology policies. Over the years since 1991, FDI norms have been further liberalized, with 100% FDI now permitted under the automatic route in many sectors.

questions.question: Match the following persons with the States where they have been appointed as Governors in March 2026. List - I (Person) List - II (State) A. Shiv Pratap Shukla 1. Telangana B. Jishnu Dev Varma 2. Maharashtra C. Nand Kishore Yadav 3. Nagaland D. Syed Ata Hasnain 4. Bihar
questions.explanation: Shiv Pratap Shukla was appointed Governor of Telangana; Jishnu Dev Varma as Governor of Maharashtra; Nand Kishore Yadav as Governor of Nagaland; and Lt. Gen. (Retd.) Syed Ata Hasnain as Governor of Bihar. Further insights: President Droupadi Murmu has appointed Governors in different States and Union Territories. The Governor of Himachal Pradesh, Shiv Pratap Shukla, has been appointed as Governor of Telangana. Telangana Governor Jishnu Dev Varma has been appointed Governor of Maharashtra. Former Bihar Assembly Speaker Nand Kishore Yadav has been appointed as Governor of Nagaland. Lieutenant General (Retired) Syed Ata Hasnain has been appointed as Governor of Bihar. The Governor of Tamil Nadu, RN Ravi, has been appointed as the Governor of West Bengal. The Governor of Tamil Nadu, RN Ravi, has been appointed as the Governor of West Bengal. The Governor of Kerala, Rajendra Vishwanath Arlekar, will discharge the functions of the Governor of Tamil Nadu. The President has accepted the resignation of the Governor of West Bengal, Dr C.V. Ananda Bose. The Lieutenant Governor of Ladakh, Kavinder Gupta, has been appointed as Governor of Himachal Pradesh. The Lieutenant Governor of Delhi Vinai Kumar Saxena, has been appointed as Lieutenant Governor of Ladakh. Former diplomat Taranjit Singh Sandhu has been appointed as Lieutenant Governor of Delhi.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about Gross National Product (GNP) calculations: I. Only the Market price of the final products should be taken into account for calculation. II. The income earned by both formal and informal economy is included in GNP. Which of the above statement(s) is/ are correct?
questions.explanation: Gross National Product (GNP) is the total value of all finished goods and services produced by a country's citizens in a given financial year, irrespective of their location. GNP also measures the output generated by a country's businesses located domestically or abroad. It can be defined as a piece of economic statistic that comprises Gross Domestic Product (GDP), and income earned by the residents from investments made overseas. GNP calculations only take market prices of Goods and services into account. (Statement I is Correct) The income earned through illegal activities and income generated by informal economy is not considered while calculating Gross National Product. (Statement II is incorrect) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p10988/image001.gif) Source: The Institute of Cost Accountants of India.

questions.question: Which of the following festival marks the completion of Hajj for Muslims?
questions.explanation: Eid ul-Zuha (also known as Eid al-Adha or the Festival of Sacrifice) marks the culmination of the annual Hajj pilgrimage to Mecca. While Muslims worldwide celebrate Eid ul-Zuha, it holds special significance for those performing the Hajj, as it occurs on the 10th day of Dhu al-Hijjah, coinciding with the completion of the main Hajj rituals, including the animal sacrifice. Explanation of Other Options: (a) Eid-al-Fitr: This festival marks the end of Ramadan, the Islamic holy month of fasting. It is celebrated on the first day of Shawwal, the month following Ramadan. (b) Shab-e-Barat: Known as the "Night of Forgiveness" or "Night of Records," it is observed in the middle of the Islamic month of Sha'ban. It is a night of prayer and seeking forgiveness for sins. (c) Milad-un-Nabi: This festival commemorates the birthday of the Prophet Muhammad. It is observed in Rabi' al-awwal, the third month in the Islamic calendar. Further insights: Hajj is one of the five pillars of Islam, an annual pilgrimage to the holy city of Mecca in Saudi Arabia that is a mandatory religious duty for all adult Muslims who are physically and financially capable of undertaking the journey. Eid ul-Zuha commemorates the willingness of Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) to sacrifice his son as an act of obedience to God's command. Muslims who are not on the Hajj pilgrimage also celebrate Eid ul-Zuha by offering special prayers, performing animal sacrifices, and sharing the meat with family, friends, and the needy.

questions.question: A single displacement reaction involves:
options: One element replacing another in a compound
questions.explanation: A single displacement reaction (also called a single replacement reaction) occurs when one element replaces another in a compound. The general form is: A+BC→AC+B where A replaces B in the compound BC. In this general reaction, element A is a metal and replaces element B, also a metal, in the compound. When the element that is doing the replacing is a nonmetal, it must replace another nonmetal in a compound, and the general equation becomes: Y+XZ→XY+Z Y is a nonmetal and replaces the nonmetal Z in the compound with X.

questions.question: Match list I (Articles) with list II (Fundamental Rights) and select the correct answer using the code given in the below list. List - I (Article)List - II (Fundamental Rights) I. Article 14A. Equality before law II. Article 19B. Right to freedom of Speech and Expression III. Article 21C. Protection of Life and Personal Liberty IV. Article 32D. Right to Constitutional Remedies
questions.explanation: All the articles are correctly matched with their corresponding Fundamental Rights as listed. I. Article 14 matches with A. Equality before law. II. Article 19 matches with B. Right to freedom of Speech and Expression (which is one of the six freedoms under Article 19). III. Article 21 matches with C. Protection of Life and Personal Liberty. IV. Article 32 matches with D. Right to Constitutional Remedies. Further Insights: These four articles are cornerstones of the Fundamental Rights guaranteed in Part III of the Indian Constitution, often considered the most vital for protecting individual liberty and ensuring a democratic society. I. Article 14: Equality before Law Two Concepts: This article contains two key concepts: Equality before the Law: A British concept, it implies the absence of any special privileges for any person and the equal subjection of all individuals to the ordinary law of the land. It is a somewhat negative concept implying no one is above the law. Equal Protection of the Laws: An American concept, this is a more positive concept that implies equality of treatment in equal circumstances. It means that the law should be applied equally to all persons who are similarly situated, and it permits reasonable classification for the purpose of legislation, but prohibits class legislation (arbitrary discrimination). Applicability: This right is available to all persons within the territory of India, including citizens, non-citizens, and legal persons like corporations. II. Article 19: Right to Freedom A Cluster of Six Freedoms: Article 19(1) guarantees six fundamental freedoms to all citizens: Freedom of Speech and Expression (clause a) Freedom of Assembly (clause b) Freedom of Association (clause c) Freedom of Movement (clause d) Freedom of Residence (clause e) Freedom to Practice any Profession, Occupation, Trade or Business (clause g) Reasonable Restrictions: These freedoms are not absolute. The Constitution allows the State to impose "reasonable restrictions" on these rights on grounds specified in clauses (2) to (6) of Article 19, such as the sovereignty and integrity of India, security of the State, public order, decency or morality, etc. Backbone of Democracy: The Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression is often called the backbone of democracy as it includes freedom of the press and the right to information. III. Article 21: Protection of Life and Personal Liberty The Heart of Fundamental Rights: Considered the most fundamental of all rights, Article 21 states, "No person shall be deprived of his life or personal liberty except according to procedure established by law." Expanded Scope: The Supreme Court of India, through numerous landmark judgments, has vastly expanded the scope of Article 21. It now includes a wide range of rights that are essential for a dignified human existence, such as: Right to a clean environment Right to livelihood Right to privacy Right to health Right to speedy trial Right to education (now also a separate Fundamental Right under Article 21A) Protection Against State Action: This right is a protection against arbitrary action by the State, not private individuals. IV. Article 32: Right to Constitutional Remedies 'Heart and Soul' of the Constitution: Dr. B. R. Ambedkar called Article 32 the "very soul of the Constitution and the very heart of it." This is because this article makes the Fundamental Rights justiciable (enforceable in a court of law). Empowering the Supreme Court: It gives every individual the right to move the Supreme Court directly for the enforcement of their Fundamental Rights. The Supreme Court is empowered to issue directions or orders or writs for this purpose. The Five Writs: The writs that the Supreme Court can issue are: Habeas Corpus: ("to have the body of") - To secure the release of a person who has been unlawfully detained. Mandamus: ("we command") - To command a public official or authority to perform a public duty. Prohibition: To prevent a lower court or tribunal from exceeding its jurisdiction. Certiorari: ("to be certified") - To quash an order of a lower court or tribunal that is illegal or without jurisdiction. Quo Warranto: ("by what authority") - To inquire into the legality of a person's claim to a public office. Similar Power for High Courts: A similar power to issue writs is vested in the High Courts under Article 226, though the High Courts' writ jurisdiction is broader as it can be invoked for the enforcement of any other legal right as well, not just Fundamental Rights.

questions.question: Under the Gupta period, who among the following wrote the book 'Hitopadesha'?
questions.explanation: Narayan Pandit was the Brahmin author of the Sanskrit treatise called Hitopadesha - a work based primarily on the Panchatantra, one of the oldest collection of stories, mainly animal fables, Hitopadesha is an Indian text in Sanskrit language consisting of fables with animal and human characters. It incorporates maxims, worldly wisdom and morals on political affairs in a simple, elegant language. Explanation of Other Options: (a) Amarismha (Amarasimha): A Sanskrit grammarian and poet, traditionally believed to be one of the Navaratnas (Nine Gems) at the court of Chandragupta II (Vikramaditya) during the Gupta period. He is famous for authoring the "Amarakosha," a thesaurus in Sanskrit. (c) Sudraka (Shudraka): An ancient Indian playwright, traditionally credited with authoring the Sanskrit play "Mrichchhakatika" (The Little Clay Cart). His period is debated but often placed around the Gupta era or slightly before/after. (d) Bhattin (This might be a misspelling or an obscure reference. If it refers to Bhatti, the author of "Bhattikavya" or "Ravanavadha," he lived around the 7th century CE, post-Gupta). Further insights: The Hitopadesha, like the Panchatantra, uses animal fables to impart lessons on ethics, polity (niti), and worldly wisdom. It is divided into four sections: Mitralabha (Gaining Friends), Suhridbheda (Causing Dissension between Friends), Vigraha (War), and Sandhi (Peace). The work has been translated into many languages and has been influential in shaping didactic literature. If the question strictly requires an author from within the Gupta period itself who wrote Hitopadesha, it might be problematic as Narayana Pandit's commonly accepted period is later. However, he is the universally accepted author of the text.

questions.question: Which Gupta ruler is traditionally credited with establishing the "Navaratnas" (nine gems) in his court?
questions.explanation: · Chandragupta II, also known by his title Vikramaditya, is traditionally credited with establishing the "Navaratnas" (Nine Gems) in his court. · This assembly of nine exceptionally talented scholars, poets, and artists is said to have adorned his court, contributing to the cultural and intellectual brilliance of the Gupta period, often referred to as India's "Golden Age." Explanation of Other Options: · Samudragupta: He was a great military conqueror and a patron of arts and learning (himself a poet and musician), but the tradition of Navaratnas is specifically associated with his son, Chandragupta II. · Skandagupta: His reign was largely occupied with defending the empire against Huna invasions, and while he maintained Gupta traditions, the Navaratnas are not linked to his court. · Kumaragupta I: He had a long and relatively peaceful reign known for administrative stability and patronage (e.g., founding of Nalanda Mahavihara), but the Navaratnas are not specifically attributed to him. Further insights: · Some of the famous personalities believed to be among the Navaratnas include the poet Kalidasa, the astronomer Varahamihira, the physician Dhanvantari, the lexicographer Amarasimha, and the grammarian Vararuchi. · While the historical accuracy of the "Navaratnas" as a formal group is debated by some scholars, the tradition highlights the flourishing of arts, literature, and sciences under Chandragupta II's patronage.

questions.question: As per 2011 Census, which of the following states has the highest tribal population in India?
questions.explanation: · As per 2011 Census, Madhya Pradesh has the highest Scheduled Tribe population (14.7 per cent). · Meghalaya has the lowest (2.5 per cent). · Total Scheduled Tribe (ST) population in the country is 10.45 crores. Madhya Pradesh has the maximum ST population -15,316,784. Further insights: It is crucial to differentiate between the state with the highest absolute number of tribals (Madhya Pradesh) and the state/UT with the highest percentage of its population as tribal (Lakshadweep at 94.8%, followed by Mizoram at 94.4%). As per the 2011 Census, the total Scheduled Tribe population in India was 104.2 million, making up 8.6% of the country's total population. The Bhil is the largest tribal group in India. The provisions related to the administration and control of Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes are mentioned in the Fifth Schedule of the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: The Earth rotates through two tidal bulges every lunar day. What is the frequency of the occurrence of high tides?
questions.explanation: Because the Earth rotates through two tidal "bulges" every lunar day, coastal areas experience two high and two low tides every 24 hours and 50 minutes. High tides occur 12 hours and 25 minutes apart. It takes six hours and 12.5 minutes for the water at the shore to go from high to low, or from low to high. A lunar day is the time it takes for a specific site on the Earth to rotate from an exact point under the moon to the same point under the moon. Unlike a solar day, however, a lunar day is 24 hours and 50 minutes. The lunar day is 50 minutes longer than a solar day because the moon revolves around the Earth in the same direction that the Earth rotates around its axis. So, it takes the Earth an extra 50 minutes to "catch up" to the moon.

questions.question: Jan Samarth Portal is a one-stop portal for __________.
questions.explanation: Prime Minister Narendra Modi launched the national portal for credit-linked government schemes 'Jan Samarth Portal' in 2022. Jan Samarth Portal is a one-stop digital portal linking government credit schemes. In November 2025, the Department of Financial Services (DFS), Ministry of Finance, has launched the Startup Common Application Journey on the Jan Samarth Portal during the Public Sector Banks Review Meeting in New Delhi. The portal provides a single digital platform for startups to access credit across all PSBs. It enables start-ups to apply for loans, compare offers and track their applications seamlessly through a unified digital journey. Backed by a Model Loan Scheme, the initiative provides loans up to ₹20 crores under the Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups (CGSS by NCGTC) which is under the aegis of the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade. The platform integrates data sources such as PAN, GST, Udyam, ITRs, and Credit Bureaus, ensuring faster processing and greater transparency. Special interest concessions are also extended to women entrepreneurs which further promotes inclusivity. This launch marks a major step towards building a collaborative, technology-driven ecosystem for India's growing startup sector, reinforcing the government's commitment to empowering entrepreneurs in alignment with the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047.

questions.question: Identify the correct statement. I. The Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna Award is now known as Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna Award. II. It was renamed in August 2019.
questions.explanation: The Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna Award is now known as the Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna Award. In August 2021, the award was renamed from Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna Award to Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna Award in honor of hockey legend Major Dhyan Chand. It is India's highest sporting honor.

questions.question: Which of the following books is INCORRECTLY paired with its respective author?
options: The God of Small Things - Arundhati Roy
questions.explanation: The Namesake is a novel by Jhumpa Lahiri, not Mira Nair (who directed the film adaptation). List of some contemporary Indian authors and their famous books: Arundhati Roy - The God of Small Things (1997), The Ministry of Utmost Happiness (2017) Jhumpa Lahiri - Interpreter of Maladies (1999), The Namesake (2003) Chetan Bhagat - Five Point Someone (2004), 2 States (2009) Amish Tripathi - The Immortals of Meluha (2010), Scion of Ikshvaku (2015) Aravind Adiga - The White Tiger (2008), Amnesty (2020) Vikram Seth - A Suitable Boy (1993), An Equal Music (1999) Anita Desai - Clear Light of Day (1980), The Village by the Sea (1982) Kiran Desai - The Inheritance of Loss (2006), Hullabaloo in the Guava Orchard (1998) Perumal Murugan - One Part Woman (2010), Poonachi (2017) Shashi Tharoor - The Great Indian Novel (1989), An Era of Darkness (2016)

questions.question: Gross Domestic Product at factor cost differs from GDP at market prices by adjusting for:
questions.explanation: · Gross Domestic Product at factor cost (GDPfc) differs from GDP at market prices (GDPmp) by adjusting for net indirect taxes. · GDP at market prices includes the impact of indirect taxes (which increase the market price) and subsidies (which decrease the market price). · GDP at factor cost represents the total income earned by the factors of production (land, labor, capital, entrepreneurship). · The relationship is: GDPfc = GDPmp - Net Indirect Taxes (where Net Indirect Taxes = Indirect Taxes - Subsidies). Explanation of Other Options: · Depreciation (Consumption of Fixed Capital): Adjusting GDP for depreciation gives Net Domestic Product (NDP). So, NDPmp = GDPmp - Depreciation, or NDPfc = GDPfc - Depreciation. · Subsidies: Subsidies are a component of 'net indirect taxes'. While they are part of the adjustment, 'net indirect taxes' is the comprehensive term for the difference. · Transfer payments: Transfer payments (like pensions, unemployment benefits) are payments made without any corresponding production of goods or services. They are not included in the calculation of GDP at all, neither at market prices nor at factor cost. Further insights: · Factor cost represents the actual cost incurred by producers. Market price is the price at which goods and services are sold in the market. · Indirect taxes (like GST) are levied by the government on goods and services, increasing their market price. Subsidies are government grants that reduce the cost of production or the market price.

questions.question: Which institutions jointly organized the First National Mediation Conference held in New Delhi in May 2025?
options: The Supreme Court of India and NITI Aayog
The Ministry of Home Affairs and the Bar Council of India
The Office of the Attorney General of India and the Ministry of Law and Justice
The Delhi High Court and the Ministry of External Affairs
questions.explanation: The First National Mediation Conference in New Delhi on 3 May 2025 was jointly organized by the Office of the Attorney General of India in association with the Ministry of Law and Justice, Government of India. This was highlighted in media coverage announcing that President Droupadi Murmu inaugurated the event and noted that these two bodies were responsible for bringing together mediation practitioners and legal stakeholders to discuss strengthening mediation frameworks in India. Further Insights The conference was inaugurated by the President of India, Droupadi Murmu, underscoring mediations national priority. It coincided with the launch of the Mediation Association of India to promote ADR practices. The event took place in New Delhi at Bharat Mandapam. Discussions focused on strengthening mediation under the Mediation Act, 2023. Senior legal officials, including the Attorney General, were key participants in the conference.

questions.question: Which part of the human brain is primarily responsible for voluntary actions and higher cognitive functions?
questions.explanation: · The cerebrum is the largest brain region, divided into two hemispheres controlling voluntary movement and cognition. · Frontal lobes in the cerebrum manage reasoning, planning, problem-solving and speech production. · Parietal lobes process sensory information (touch, temperature, pain). · Cerebellum regulates balance and coordination, not higher cognition. · Medulla oblongata controls involuntary functions like breathing and heart rate. · Hypothalamus regulates homeostasis, hunger, thirst and endocrine functions.

questions.question: Which of the following laws describes the amount of heat produced in a conductor due to the passage of electric current?
questions.explanation: · Joule's law (specifically Joule's first law of heating) describes the amount of heat produced in a conductor due to the passage of electric current. · It states that the heat (H) generated in a conductor is directly proportional to the square of the current (I), the resistance (R) of the conductor, and the time (t) for which the current flows, i.e., H = I²Rt. Explanation of Other Options: · Ohm's law: This law relates voltage (V), current (I), and resistance (R) in an electrical circuit (V = IR). It does not directly describe heat generation, though it can be used in conjunction with Joule's law (e.g., H = VIt or H = V²t/R). · Ampere's law (Ampère's circuital law): This law relates the integrated magnetic field around a closed loop to the electric current passing through the loop. It deals with magnetic fields produced by currents, not thermal effects. · Faraday's law (Faraday's law of induction): This law describes how a changing magnetic field induces an electromotive force (emf) in a conductor. It pertains to electromagnetic induction. Further insights: · The heating effect of electric current is a fundamental concept in physics and has many practical applications, such as in electric heaters, toasters, incandescent bulbs, and fuses. · It also represents energy loss in power transmission lines, which is why high voltages are used for long-distance transmission to reduce current (and thus I²R losses).

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding Indian folk theatre forms: a. Yakshagana is a traditional theatre form from Karnataka that combines dance, music, and elaborate costumes to depict stories from epics and Puranas. b. Bhand Pather is a satirical folk theatre form from Kashmir, which blends music, dance, and mimicry to comment on social issues. c. Jatra, a popular folk-theatre form from Maharashtra, is known for its inclusion of the Lavani dance form. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement a is correct. Yakshagana is indeed the classical folk theatre of Karnataka, renowned for its grand costumes and epic storytelling. Statement b is correct. Bhand Pather is the traditional folk theatre of Kashmir, which uses satire and parody performed by jesters known as 'Bhands'. Statement c is incorrect. This statement incorrectly combines two different folk forms. Jatra is the folk theatre of Bengal/Eastern India. The folk theatre from Maharashtra that is known for its inclusion of the Lavani dance form is called Tamasha.

questions.question: Part IX A of the Constitution deals with which of the following?
questions.explanation: · Municipalities are governed under Part IX A (Articles 243P - 243ZG), introduced by the 74th Amendment. · It empowers urban local bodies for governance, planning, and financial authority. · Part IX covers Panchayats; Cooperative Societies and Public Service Commissions are governed elsewhere. · Municipalities include Municipal Corporations, Municipal Councils, and Nagar Panchayats. · It mandates regular elections, reservation of seats, and devolution of powers to urban local bodies.

questions.question: The cost price of two articles is Rs. 2100. If one article is sold at 15% loss and the other article is sold at 20% profit. On the whole, he has no profit, no loss. Find the cost price of each article.
questions.explanation: Let the C.P of the article sold at 15% loss be Rs. X C.P of article sold at 20% profit = Rs. 2100 - X X * 85/100 + (2100 - X) * 120/100 = 2100 17X/20 + 12600/5 - 6X/5 = 2100 17X/20 + 2520 - 24X/20 = 2100 7X/20 = 420 X = 1200 2100 - X = Rs. 900 Required C.P of each article = Rs. 1200, Rs. 900

questions.question: Shweta completed a journey in 7.8 hours. If she completed 20% of the journey at 50 km/h and remaining journey at 60 km/h, then find the distance travelled by her.
questions.explanation: Let distance = 100D Total time = 7.8 hours 20D/50 + 80D/60 = 7.8 2D/5 + 4D/3 = 7.8 26D/15 = 7.8 D = 4.5 Total distance = 450 km

questions.question: When the sides of a cube are made three times of the original side, the volume of the new cube will become:
questions.explanation: Let side of the original cube = 'a' unit Volume of the original cube = a3 Volume of the new cube = (3a)3= 27a3 Volume of the new cube = 27 * Volume of the original cube

questions.question: A's price is Rs 120 more than B's price and C's price is Rs 30 more than D's. If the ratio of A's and C's price is 3:4 and the ratio of B's and D's price is 2:3, then the price of A.
questions.explanation: A: C 3: 4............. ( X a) B: D 2: 3............( X b) According to question 3a-2b=120 4a-3b=30 ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p14785/image059.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p14785/image060.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p14785/image061.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p14785/image062.png) A : C : B : D 30 : 40 : 26 : 39 (30-26) =4 4≡120 1≡30 Therefore, A=900

questions.question: In a 600 m race A beats B by 45 m or 12 seconds. B's time over the course is:
questions.explanation: B runs 45 m in 12 sec. B covers 600 m in (600 * 12/45) = 160 sec

questions.question: The number between 8000 and 9000 that is divisible by each of 16, 27, 45 and 20 is:
questions.explanation: Required number must be a multiple of LCM(16, 27, 45, 20). 16 = 24 27 = 33 45 = 32 X 5 20 = 22 X 5 LCM = 24 X 33 X 5 = 16 X 27 X 5 = 2160 Find the multiple of 2160 between 8000 and 9000: 2160 X 3 = 6480 (too small) 2160 X 4 = 8640 (fits) 2160 X 5 = 10800 (too large) So, the number is 8640

questions.question: In a group of 10 participants with average height 175 cm, the average height of first 4 participants is 172 cm and the average height of last 4 participants is 180 cm. If the height of 6th participant is 161 cm, then what is the height of 5th participant?
questions.explanation: Total height of 10 participants = 10 * 175 = 1750 cm Total height of first 4 participants = 4 * 172 = 688 cm Total height of last 4 participants = 4 * 180 = 720 cm Height of 6th participant = 161 cm Height of 5th participant = 1750 - (688 + 720 + 161) = 181 cm

questions.question: If AD is altitude to the hypotenuse of a right ∆ ABC, AB = 15 cm and AC = 8 cm, then find the length of AD (to the nearest whole number).
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p14265/image010.jpg) ar (∆ABC) = ½ * base * altitude = ½ * AB * AC = ½ * AD * BC AD = AB * AC/BC = 120/17 ≈ 7 cm

questions.question: Which of the following is a terminating decimal?
questions.explanation: A fraction a/b in lowest terms, and b has only prime factors 2 and/or 5. a: 21/80 is already in lowest terms, 80 = 24 X 5, so 21/80 is a terminating decimal. b: 75 = 3 X 52 includes 3, so 13/75 is non-terminating recurring. c: 42 = 2 X 3 X 7 includes 3 and 7, so 11/42 is non-terminating recurring. d: 18 = 2 X 32 includes 3, so 7/18 is non-terminating recurring.

questions.question: PQRS is a cyclic quadrilateral such that PQ is the diameter of the circle and ∠PSR = 110°, then what is the measure of ∠QPR?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p13590/image036.png) PQRS is a cyclic quadrilateral which means ∠PSR + ∠PQR = 180° ∠PQR = 180° - 110° = 70° We know that: Angle in a semi-circle is always right-angle. ∠PRQ = 90° Now in ∆PRQ: ∠PQR + ∠PRQ + ∠QPR = 180° ∠QPR = 180° - 70° - 90° ∠QPR = 20°

questions.question: The alumni fund at Deccan College invests Rs. 27,000 at 10% p.a. for 3 years. Find the excess of compound interest over simple interest (compounded annually).

Rishi got 60% marks and passed the exam while Karan got 59% marks and failed the exam. The passing marks is an integer and the maximum marks of the exam are 200. If Suman scored more than Karan but less than Rishi, find the marks scored by Suman.
questions.explanation: Rishi got = 200 X 60/100 = 120 Karan got = 200 X 59/100 = 118 Suman got between Rishi and Karan's marks So, Suman got 119 marks

questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p15484/image004.png)

questions.question: The ratio of ages of B and C 8 years ago was 7: 4 and the average of present ages of A and C is 33 years. If A's age 3 years ago is equal to B's age 3 years hence, then find the present age of B.
questions.explanation: Let the present age of B and C be (7X + 8) years and (4X + 8) years respectively. The present age of A = 33 * 2 - 4X - 8 = (58 - 4X) years A's present age - 3 = B's present age + 3 58 - 4X - 3 = 7X + 8 + 3 11X = 44 X = 4 Hence, B's present age = 7 * 4 + 8 = 36 years

questions.question: Dev, Esha and Farhan initially have money in the ratio 7 : 9 : 11. Dev gives 10% of his money to Esha. Then Farhan gives ₹1880 to Esha. After these transfers, Dev and Farhan have equal amounts. What was Dev's initial amount?
questions.explanation: Let initial amounts be 7k, 9k, 11k. Dev gives 10% of 7k = 0.7k to Esha: New amounts of Dev = 7k − 0.7k = 6.3k, Esha = 9k + 0.7k = 9.7k, Farhan = 11k. Farhan gives ₹1880 to Esha: Esha = 9.7k + 1880 Farhan = 11k − 1880. according to question, Dev = Farhan => 6.3k = 11k − 1880. => 11k − 6.3k = 1880 => 4.7k = 1880 => k = 400. Dev's initial amount = 7k => 7 X 400 = Rs. 2800.

questions.question: If tan θ = 2 + √3, then tan θ + cot θ = ?
A sum of Rs. 4000 amounts to Rs. 5200 in 2 years at certain rate of simple interest. Find the interest obtained after 3 years on Rs. 2000 at same rate of simple interest.

A can do a piece of work in 10 days. B is 25% more efficient than A. If C alone takes 12 days to complete the work, then find in how many days the work can be completed when all of them work together.
questions.explanation: Number of days taken by A = 10 Number of days taken by B = 10 x 100/125 = 8 Number of days taken by C = 12 Let total work = LCM of 10, 8, and 12 = 120 units Time taken by all three to complete the work together = 120/ [(120/10) + (120/8) + (120/12)] = 120/(12 + 15 + 10) = 3(9/37) days

questions.question: A sample A contains two types of sugar costing Rs.70 per kg and Rs.20 per kg in the ratio 3:7, and sample B contains the same two sugars in the ratio 2:3. In what ratio should samples A and B be mixed so that the final mixture contains sugar costing Rs.70 per kg and Rs.20 per kg in the ratio 5:9?
questions.explanation: Let the quantities of A and B to be mixed be m kg and n kg respectively. Then in the final mixture, Quantity of Rs.70 sugar = (3/10)m + (2/5)n kg Quantity of Rs.20 sugar = (7/10)m + (3/5)n kg According to question, [(3/10)m + (2/5)n] / [(7/10)m + (3/5)n] = 5 / 9 => [3m + 4n] / [7m + 6n] = 5 / 9 => 9(3m + 4n) = 5(7m + 6n) => 27m + 36n = 35m + 30n => 36n − 30n = 35m − 27m => 6n = 8m => m/n = 6/8 = 3/4

questions.question: Find the height of an equilateral triangle whose perimeter is 408 cm.
questions.explanation: Side of the equilateral triangle = 408/3 = 136 cm Hence, height of the equilateral triangle = (√3/2) X Side = (√3/2) X 136 = 68√3 cm

questions.question: What value should come in place of the question mark? [(27 + 25) X 2] ÷ 4 + ? = 38
The average of six numbers (including x) is 24. The average of three specific numbers y, z, w is 18, and the average of x, y, z, w is 22. What is the average of the five numbers excluding x?
questions.explanation: Sum of all six = 6 X 24 = 144 Sum of y, z, w = 3 X 18 = 54 Sum of x + y + z + w = 4 X 22 = 88 => x + 54 = 88 => x = 34 Sum of the other five (exclude x) = 144 − 34 = 110 Average = 110/5 = 22

questions.question: In a cuboid, the length is 12 cm more than its width and the width is twice its height. If the sum of length, width and height is 57 cm, then what is the volume of the cuboid?
questions.explanation: Let the height of the cuboid be 'a' cm Width = 2a cm Length = 2a + 12 cm A/Q a + 2a + 2a + 12 = 57 5a = 45 a = 9 cm The volume of the cuboid = L * B * H = 9 * 18 * 30 = 4860 cm3

questions.question: The angle of elevation from a man standing on a point on the ground of the top of a pole is 30˚. The distance between the man and the foot of pole is 120cm. What is the length of the pole?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p21418/image003.jpg) In ∆ABC - tan30˚ = AB/BC 1/√3 = AB/120 AB = 120/√3 AB = 40√3cm

questions.question: Shani and Mangal are partners in a traveling business. Shani invests Rs. 12600 for 35 months and Mangal invests Rs. 18900 for 14 months. Out of total profit of Rs. 20880, what will be the share of profit of Shani?
questions.explanation: Ratio of investment of Shani and Mangal= (12600 * 35): (18900 * 14) = 5: 3 Hence, share of profit of Shani = 5/8 * 20880 = Rs. 13050

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. Even though Anika knew she had no chance of winning the contract, she decided to make a last desperate attempt anyway.
options: take a shot in the dark
questions.explanation: The idiom "take a shot in the dark" means to make a guess or attempt without knowing the outcome, fitting the sentence perfectly. Hence, option (a) is correct. Options b), c), and d) are incorrect constructions and not standard idioms.

questions.question: A sentence is provided in direct speech. From the four given options, choose the one that most accurately conveys the sentence in its corresponding indirect speech. My sister said, "Please don't leave the lights on when you go out."
options: My sister told me that I didn't leave the lights on when I went out.
My sister requested me not to leave the lights on when I went out.
My sister requested that I don't leave the lights on when I go out.
My sister said me to not leave the lights on when I go out.
questions.explanation: Correct option first: Option b is correct because "please don't.." is a polite request in the negative form. Rule: For negative requests/commands, use "requested/asked + object + not to + verb." Example: "Please don't shout," she said → She requested me not to shout. "Please don't leave the lights on" becomes "requested me not to leave the lights on." "When you go out" can shift based on the reporting verb; "when I went out" is acceptable after past reporting. Final correct sentence: My sister requested me not to leave the lights on when I went out. Incorrect options: ·a. Changes the meaning into a statement of fact (as if I already didn't leave the lights on). ·c. Uses "don't/go" (present) instead of a proper reported structure; it also misses the clean "not to" pattern. ·d. Uses wrong structure ("said me to") which is grammatically incorrect.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Vivacious
questions.explanation: Vivacious: attractively lively and animated (typically used for a woman). Listless: (of a person or their manner) lacking energy or enthusiasm. As per the meanings, 'listless' is the correct antonym. Hence, option (a) is the answer. Animated: full of life or excitement; lively. Lively: full of life and energy; active and outgoing. Jolly: happy and cheerful.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined group of words. A person who believes pleasure is the highest good in life may ignore deeper values.
questions.explanation: The underlined phrase is "person who believes pleasure is the highest good in life." a) Nihilist - One who believes life is meaningless. b) Hedonist - One who seeks pleasure as the ultimate goal. c) Stoic - One who endures pain without showing emotion. d) Epicurean - One devoted to refined sensual pleasure, especially food. Hence, option (b) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. Despite challenges, they managed to complete the project successfully before the deadline ended.
questions.explanation: The phrase "pulled off" means to succeed in doing something difficult or unexpected, which correctly fits the sentence. Pulled off - Correct, as it means to accomplish something successfully despite difficulties (completing the project before the deadline). Pulled over - Means to stop a vehicle at the side of the road (e.g., The police officer asked him to pull over). Pulled out - Means to withdraw from a situation or event (e.g., The company pulled out of the deal). Pulled up - Means to stop a vehicle or to move something upward (e.g., She pulled up in front of the house). Thus, the correct substitution is "pulled off." Hence, option (a) is the answer.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into three segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. If the sentence is error free, mark 'No error' as your answer. Sneha is representing/ our school's tennis team/ for the last five years.
questions.explanation: As the sentence talks about an action which started in the past and still continues in the present, it qualifies for the present perfect continuous tense. According to the structure of the sentence in the present perfect continuous tense ('Subject+ has/have + been + -ing form of verb + object + since/for'), 'is' will be replaced by 'has been'. The correct sentence: Sneha has been representing our school's tennis team for the last five years. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one word substitute for the underlined group of words in the given sentence. Her skill and sensitivity in dealing with difficult issues while handling delicate diplomatic matters earned her respect among world leaders.
questions.explanation: Succinctness: The quality of being concise and expressing ideas clearly in a few words. Tactfulness: Sensitivity and skill in dealing with others or with difficult issues; diplomacy. Quiescence: A state of quietness or inactivity; dormancy. Equanimity: Mental calmness, composure, and evenness of temper. Among the given options, 'tactfulness' can be used as a one word substitute for the underlined group of words in the sentence. Hence, the correct answer is option (b).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word. What is it about Harry Potter that so engrosses children?
questions.explanation: Engrosses: absorb all the attention or interest of. Engages: occupy or attract (someone's interest or attention). Both words are similar in meaning. So, option (a) is the answer. Irritates: showing or feeling slight anger; annoyed. Guesses: estimate or conclude (something) without sufficient information to be sure of being correct. Confronts: come face to face with (someone) with hostile or argumentative intent.

questions.question: In the following question, a sentence has been given in Active Voice. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Passive Voice. Has Ramesh completed the task?
options: Has the task completed by Ramesh?
Has the task been completed by Ramesh?
Have the task been completed by Ramesh?
Has the task being completed by Ramesh?
questions.explanation: · The correct answer is (b) Has the task been completed by Ramesh? · The subject of the Active Voice is changed into object of the Passive voice. Also, the object of the Active Voice is changed is changed into subject of the Passive Voice. · The usage of a helping verb ['been' here] is necessary while changing the voice of the sentence from Active to Passive. · Preposition "by" is used before the object made in Passive Voice. However, it is optional in some cases. · The question in Active voice is an interrogative sentence. The answer to this question will be either "yes" or "no". · These types of sentences usually begin with a helping verb like: o Have o Has o Had · The format of the sentence which begins with "Have", "Has" or "Had" in Active voice is: Have/Has/Had + Subject + V3 + Object? · Therefore, in Passive voice, the format will be: Have/Has/Had + Subject (Objective case) + been + V3 + by + Object (Subjective case). · For example: o Has Rohit written the letter? (Active) o Has the letter been written by Rohit? (Passive) · The options except (b) don't follow the correct grammatical format of the Passive Voice. · Thus, the correct answer is: (b) Has the task been completed by Ramesh? V1 -> Base form/simple form of verb, V2 -> Past form of verb (-ed form or irregular verb), V3 -> Past participle form of verb (-ed, -d, -t, -en, or -n), V4 -> Continuous form of verb / Present participle form of verb (-ing form), V5 -> Third person singular (V1+ -es or -s)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom. With his personal assistant at his beck and call, Adam was able to get a lot of work done in the office today.
options: Ready to perform tasks for someone immediately
Always attend the phone as soon as it rings
questions.explanation: The phrase 'beck and call' means always having to be ready to obey someone's orders immediately. Hence, (a) is correct.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined group of words in the given sentence. The organization became a society governed by old people, where innovation stalled and fresh ideas struggled to gain traction.
questions.explanation: Gerontocracy: a state, society, or group governed by old people. Hence, option (c) is the answer. Kakistocracy: government by the least suitable or competent citizens of a state. Plutocracy: government by the wealthy. Autocracy: government by a single person or small group that has unlimited power or authority.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word: LASSITUDE
questions.explanation: Lassitude - a state of physical or mental weariness; lack of energy. Lethargy - a lack of energy and enthusiasm; sluggishness. Both words describe a state of weariness, sluggishness, and lack of energy. Hence, option c) is the correct answer. Energy - the strength and vitality required for sustained physical or mental activity. Attitude - a settled way of thinking or feeling about someone or something. Strength - the quality or state of being physically strong.

questions.question: Select the incorrectly spelt word.
questions.explanation: It should be spelled "separate". It means "forming or viewed as a unit apart or by itself". ("dilemma" is a difficult choice, "questionnaire" is a survey of questions, "exaggerate" is to overstate.) Hence, option (d) is the answer.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. Being toxically positive can also cause feelings of guilt. B. It sends a message that if you aren't finding a way to feel positive - even in the face of tragedy - you are doing something wrong. C. Rather than being able to share genuine human emotions and gain unconditional support, people who are faced with toxic positivity find their feelings dismissed, ignored, or outright invalidated. D. Too much positivity is toxic because it can harm people who are going through difficult times.
questions.explanation: After analysing the sentences, we can conclude that sentence D introduces the core idea regarding "too much positivity being toxic", setting the foundation for the discussion. Next, sentence C logically follows, explaining the consequences of toxic positivity, specifically how it dismisses or invalidates people's feelings. Sentence A then connects with sentence B, as they form a pair. Sentence A discusses how being toxically positive can cause guilt, which is further explained in sentence B, where it is stated that toxic positivity sends the harmful message that if you're not finding a way to feel positive, you're doing something wrong. The correct order of sentences is DCAB. Hence, (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Rearrange the following sentences to form a coherent paragraph: 1) Adding protein and fibre helps the body stay full for a longer time. 2) Such meals raise blood sugar quickly, but it drops faster and triggers hunger again. 3) Many people feel hungry soon after a large meal, which seems confusing at first. 4) This can happen when the meal is high in refined carbohydrates and low in protein.
questions.explanation: Correct Option First: b is correct, and the right order is 3→ 4 → 2 → 1. Sentence 1 states the situation. Sentence 2 gives the reason type of meal. Sentence 3 explains the effect that leads to hunger. Sentence 4 provides the practical fix. Rule (simple): Situation → cause → explanation → solution. Example: "I get tired → I sleep late → body recovers less → sleep earlier." · Option a is incorrect because it starts with "This can happen.." (2) before clearly stating what is happening (1). · Option c is incorrect because it jumps to the sugar effect (3) before naming the meal type (2). · Option d is incorrect because it begins with the explanation (3) without introducing the situation first (1). Final correct paragraph: 1 → 2 → 3 → 4. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.question: Select the correct spelling of the underlined word. The unsettling news about the pandemic caused a wave of nauusea among the population.
questions.explanation: Among the given options, the correct spelling of the underlined word is 'nausea'. Nausea: A feeling of sickness with an inclination to vomit (used figuratively in the given sentence). Hence, option (a) is the correct answer. Unsettling: Causing discomfort and anxiety; disturbing.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the most appropriate adverb: The technician checked the wiring ____ before restoring the power supply.
questions.explanation: Option b is correct because it describes how the technician checked (an action), so we need an adverb: "carefully." Rule (Adverb for actions): Adverbs modify verbs and usually answer "how." Example: "He drove carefully in the rain." · Option a is wrong because "careful" is an adjective, not used to describe "checked." · Option c is wrong because "care" is a noun/verb, not an adverb. · Option d is wrong because "more careful" is not an adverb form here. Final correct sentence: The technician checked the wiring carefully before restoring the power supply.

questions.question: Complete the following collocation. The project was completed in ______ time due to the team's efficiency.
questions.explanation: The phrase "record time" is a strong collocation, meaning something was completed faster than usual or expected. Record time - Correct, as it means the project was completed very quickly due to efficiency. Hesitant time - Incorrect, as "hesitant" means uncertain or slow, which contradicts efficiency. Weak time - Incorrect, as "weak" does not logically fit with the idea of project completion. Mild time - Incorrect, as "mild" refers to something moderate or gentle, which is unrelated to speed. Thus, the correct answer is "record." So, the complete sentence is: The project was completed in record time due to the team's efficiency. Hence, option (a) is the answer.

questions.question: Which sentence uses onomatopoeia?
options: The old gate complained in the wind.
Her words were knives in the meeting.
The hot oil went "sizzle" as the onions dropped in.
He ran as fast as a bullet train.
questions.explanation: Correct option is The hot oil went "sizzle" as the onions dropped in. (Option c) because "sizzle" is a sound-word. Onomatopoeia means a word that imitates a real sound. Example: "buzz," "thud," "clang." Option a is wrong because "complained" gives a human action to a gate (personification), not a sound-word. Option b is wrong because "words were knives" is a direct comparison (metaphor), not a sound imitation. Option d is wrong because it compares using "as" (simile), not sound imitation. Final sentence: The hot oil went "sizzle" as the onions dropped in.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice. The documents had been prepared by the assistant. The revisions had been made by the editor.
options: The assistant had prepared the documents. The editor had made the revisions.
The assistant was prepared the documents. The editor was made the revisions.
The assistant had been preparing the documents. The editor had been making the revisions.
The assistant will be preparing the documents. The editor had made the revisions.
questions.explanation: The subject of the Passive Voice is changed into the object of the Active Voice. Also, the object of the Passive Voice is changed into the subject of the Active Voice. The removal of the "to be" verb [been] and the preposition "by" is necessary while changing the voice of the sentence from Passive to Active. The Passive voice in the given question is in Past Perfect. Thus, the Active voice will also be in Past Perfect. In Passive voice, the format is: Object of the Active voice (Subjective case) + had + been + V3 + by + Subject of the Active voice (Objective case). Thus, the format of Active voice is: Subject + had + V3 + Object. For example: She had been called by me. (Passive Voice) I had called her. (Active Voice) The options except (a) don't follow the correct grammatical format of the Active Voice. Therefore, the correct answer is: The assistant had prepared the documents. The editor had made the revisions. Hence, (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the sentence, "Solar, wind, hydroelectric, and geothermal power provide cleaner alternatives that produce little to no greenhouse gas emissions," the clause "that produce little to no greenhouse gas emissions" is an example of which type of clause?
questions.explanation: In the sentence, "Solar, wind, hydroelectric, and geothermal power provide cleaner alternatives that produce little to no greenhouse gas emissions," the clause "that produce little to no greenhouse gas emissions" gives additional information about the noun "alternatives." Because it modifies a noun and begins with the relative pronoun "that," it functions as an adjective (relative) clause. Option a: Noun clause • A noun clause acts as a subject, object, or complement (e.g., What he said is true). • The clause here describes a noun rather than acting as one. Option b: Adverb clause of result • Adverb clauses modify verbs and show cause, time, condition, or result (e.g., so that, because). • The clause does not explain a result; it describes the type of alternatives. Option d: Coordinate clause • Coordinate clauses are independent clauses joined by conjunctions like and, but, or. The given clause is dependent and cannot stand alone. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.question: Which metaphor is used to describe the role of fossil fuels in past industrial growth?
options: Fossil fuels as a "bridge" to modernization
Fossil fuels as the "engine" of industrial growth
Fossil fuels as a "pillar" of sustainability
Fossil fuels as a "shield" against climate change
questions.explanation: The passage states that fossil fuels powered industrial growth for over a century. The metaphor "engine" symbolically represents the driving force behind industrial expansion and economic development. Just as an engine powers a machine, fossil fuels enabled industries, transportation, and infrastructure to function and grow. The other options are incorrect because the passage does not portray fossil fuels as a bridge, pillar of sustainability, or shield against climate change. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.question: What is the closest antonym for "affordable" as used in the line "making clean energy more affordable and accessible"?
questions.explanation: In the passage, "affordable" means reasonably priced or within financial reach for most people. The closest opposite in meaning is "costly," which indicates something expensive or difficult to afford. The other options do not express the opposite of price: scarce refers to availability, inefficient relates to performance, and limited refers to quantity or scope. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.question: What is the tone of the passage?
questions.explanation: The passage examines the environmental harm caused by fossil fuels and explains practical solutions such as renewable energy adoption, energy efficiency, and policy measures. While it acknowledges challenges and the need for global cooperation, it emphasizes progress, benefits, and future sustainability. This balanced evaluation combined with hope for improvement reflects an analytical approach and a cautiously optimistic tone. The passage is not alarmist, indifferent, or dismissive. Option a: Alarmist and pessimistic An alarmist tone exaggerates danger and creates panic, while a pessimistic tone suggests hopelessness. The passage does mention environmental threats, but it focuses on solutions and progress. Option c: Indifferent and neutral An indifferent tone shows lack of concern. The passage clearly stresses urgency, environmental protection, and public health benefits, showing strong engagement with the issue. Option d: Critical and dismissive A dismissive tone rejects ideas or undermines efforts. Instead, the passage supports renewable energy, policy action, and sustainable practices rather than rejecting them.Top of FormBottom of Form Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.question: What does the phrase "energy security" imply in the context of the passage?
options: Protecting energy resources through military means
Storing fossil fuels for emergency use only
Limiting energy consumption to conserve reserves
Ensuring stable, reliable, and sustainable access to energy supplies
questions.explanation: In the passage, "energy security" refers to maintaining a dependable energy supply that meets present and future needs without disruption. The transition to renewable energy, improved storage, and diversified sources helps ensure consistent availability while supporting sustainability. The other options misinterpret the phrase: it does not refer to military protection, emergency fuel storage alone, or restricting consumption. Hence, option (d) is correct.
